settings.text: A first visit that feels organized before you arrive.
Bring records, a current medication list, and anything you have noticed at home. We will guide the rest.

settings.title: Your first visit, step by step.
settings.description: A simple visit flow helps your pet settle in and helps the team make useful recommendations.

settings.title: Send records if you have them
settings.description: Previous vaccines, lab work, medications, and surgery notes help us avoid guesswork.
settings.features:
+ Email records before the visit
+ Bring medication names and doses

settings.title: Check in with your pet secure
settings.description: Dogs should arrive on a leash and cats should arrive in a carrier.
settings.features:
+ Tell us if your pet is nervous
+ We can adjust the room setup

settings.title: Talk through health and behavior
settings.description: The veterinarian examines your pet, answers questions, and explains what is recommended.

settings.title: Leave with next steps
settings.description: You will know what to watch for, when to return, and how to reach us with follow-up questions.

settings.title: Helpful documents before your appointment.
settings.description: Upload your own forms after setup, or use this section as a simple new-patient checklist.

settings.title: New client form
settings.description: Basic household details, preferred contact method, and consent information.

settings.title: Pet medical history
settings.description: A place to note diet, medications, allergies, symptoms, and prior care.

settings.title: First-visit checklist
settings.description: What to bring, when to arrive, and how to prepare nervous pets.

settings.title: First-visit questions.
settings.description: A few details that make the appointment easier for everyone.

settings.title: Bring with you
settings.text:
Medical records

Medication list

Stool sample if requested

Questions you want answered

settings.question: My cat hates the carrier. What should I do?
settings.answer: Leave the carrier out at home before the visit when possible. Add familiar bedding and call us if your cat needs extra travel guidance.

settings.question: My dog is nervous at the vet. Can you help?
settings.answer: Yes. Tell us before arrival so we can choose a calmer room setup and move at a pace that keeps the visit useful.

settings.question: Will my pet get vaccines on the first visit?
settings.answer: Maybe. The doctor will review records, health status, lifestyle, and age before recommending vaccines.

settings.question: When do I pay?
settings.answer: Payment is due at the time of service. We explain estimates before care begins whenever treatment choices are involved.

settings.text: New patients are welcome by appointment. Call or send a request and we will help you get started.
